items.note: The fuselage is called Advance+, not Advance 20

items.correct: Fatty mast for 1500+ span wings AND heavy riders
items.note: Not just for winging — it's for high-load situations

PNG.name: PNG (Pump N Glide)
PNG.tagline: World-class pump foil, legendary light-wind glide machine
PNG.character: Big span, low stall speed, not super turny but incredible glide. Best beginner and light-wind option in the AXIS lineup. Uses Red fuselage.
PNG.who_its_for: Beginners learning to foil, pump foiling athletes, downwind beginners, light-wind winging
PNG.not_ideal_for: Aggressive carving, choppy conditions, racing speed

PNG 1310.highlight: World record holder for non-stop pump foiling
PNG 1310.notes: Legendary pump foil. James Casey's choice. Best for light-wind anything.
PNG 1310.weight_range: All weights, lighter riders get more out of it

PNG 1300.highlight: James Casey's recommendation for learning downwind
PNG 1300.notes: Big span catches small wind swell. Best entry point for downwind progression. Step 1 in the downwind path.

PNG 1150.notes: Great light-wind all-rounder, very stable platform

PNG 1010.notes: Stepping stone between large and mid-range

PNG 910.notes: Great for lighter riders wanting more maneuverability

expert_quotes.quote: Start downwind on the PNG 1300 — big span catches small wind swell. It'll teach you to catch bumps.

community_insights: PNG 1310 is the world record pump foil — it's not just marketing
Danny Perez: Went from standard to Advance+ fuselage and it made the foils so much more responsive for such large spans
Shaun Henderson: 1150 is what I learned to wing and dock start with — Advance fuselage is much better for pumping

BSC.name: BSC (Broad Spectrum Carve)
BSC.tagline: The best all-rounder — does everything well
BSC.character: Early pop-up, maneuverable, forgiving. Not as fast as HPS/ART but more user-friendly. True all-rounder that works for everything.
BSC.who_its_for: Beginners to intermediates wanting versatility. Anyone who does multiple disciplines on one wing.

fuselage_guide.large_BSC: Red fuselage (BSC 1060, 1120 — thicker, larger span wings)
fuselage_guide.small_BSC: Black fuselage (BSC 890 and below — thinner profile wings)

BSC 1120.notes: Best starting point for riders 90kg+. Uses Red fuselage.

BSC 1060.notes: Sweet spot for 75-90kg riders learning to wing. Uses Red fuselage.

BSC 970.notes: Under 75kg starting point. Uses Red fuselage.

BSC 890.notes: Transition to Black fuselage territory. More agile than larger BSC.

BSC 810.notes: Very popular size for intermediate riders stepping up. Excellent kite foil.

BSC 740.notes: High-wind, lighter riders, or those wanting more maneuverability

community_insights: BSC 1060 is the go-to recommendation for 75-90kg beginners — it forgives mistakes while building skills
BSC 810 is one of the most popular sizes across all riders — great stepping stone to HPS

HPS.name: HPS (High Performance Speed)
HPS.tagline: Speed + accessibility — the natural next step from BSC
HPS.character: Fast, glidey, needs more speed to get up. Natural stepping stone from BSC. Pairs well with Progressive and Speed rear wings.
HPS.who_its_for: Intermediate to advanced riders wanting speed and efficiency without going full high-aspect
warnings: HPS 880 can have pitch control challenges for some riders — if you're fighting it up/down, that's normal early on
Needs more water speed to get up compared to BSC — make sure your technique is solid first

HPS 1050.notes: Massive lift for SUP, surprisingly accessible

HPS 880.notes: Some riders report pitch control challenges initially. Use Advance fuselage and good mast for best results.

community_insights: HPS 880 pitch issues are real but manageable — give it time and focus on smooth body movements
Great stepping stone between BSC and ART — speed-focused riders love it

ART.name: ART (AXIS Research Team)
ART.tagline: Pinnacle high-aspect performance — not for choppy water
ART.character: Frictionless glide, reduced chord, high aspect. Needs Power Carbon mast and Advance fuselage for best results. NOT for turbulent water.
ART.who_its_for: Advanced winging and prone riders who have mastered HPS. Smooth water specialists.
warnings: NOT for beginners or choppy water — this is an advanced tool
ART in turbulent/choppy conditions is frustrating — Spitfire or ART V2 handles chop better
Requires Power Carbon mast for proper performance

ART 999.notes: Most popular ART size — the benchmark

community_insights: Yvon Labarthe: ART V2 1099 has 25% less glide than FB 1160 — Fireball wins for pumping
ART V2 939 turns 10-15% better than FB 1000 — better for wing/tow
ART V2 879 behind a boat in towing — turns like crazy

ART Pro.name: ART Pro
ART Pro.character: Stiffer, more responsive than standard ART. For advanced riders who can dictate the foil, not react to it.
ART Pro.who_its_for: Elite downwind racers, Kai Lenny-level riders

ART Pro 1201.highlight: Great 2nd/3rd wing for downwind progression after PNG 1300
ART Pro 1201.notes: Step 2 in downwind progression. Significantly more demanding than PNG.

ART Pro 951.highlight: Kai Lenny's race wing (5th at Molokai to Oahu)
ART Pro 951.notes: Elite racing performance. Step 3 in downwind progression (or alternative to Fireball).

community_insights: Kai Lenny used the ART Pro 951 to finish 5th at Molokai to Oahu — says everything about its performance

ART V2.name: ART V2 (2024)
ART V2.tagline: More forgiving ART — Spitfire Pro character
ART V2.character: Spitfire's turn + forgiveness with ART's glide and speed. Better for UK/choppy conditions. Great intermediate downwind option.
ART V2.who_its_for: Advanced riders who want ART performance with more forgiveness. Choppy water riders.
community_insights: Yvon: ART v2 turns 5-10% better rail-to-rail but Fireball has 25% more glide — pick your priority
ART V2 879 is excellent for tow/wing, not for pumping
Better than original ART in choppy UK conditions

Spitfire.name: Spitfire
Spitfire.tagline: Wave riding specialist — sharp turns, handles turbulence
Spitfire.character: Sharp, smooth turns. Handles turbulence better than ART. Pair with Advance fuselages + small progressive rears for max carve.
Spitfire.who_its_for: Wave riders (winging, prone), choppy downwind riders, UK-style conditions

Spitfire 1180.highlight: Very popular, great for downwind progression
Spitfire 1180.notes: Step 2 in downwind progression (alongside ART Pro 1201). Handles chop better than ART.

Spitfire 1100.notes: Light wind wing, SUP, choppy downwind

Spitfire 1030.notes: Light wind wing, SUP, choppy downwind

Spitfire 960.notes: UK prone conditions, less advanced winging

community_insights: Spitfire 1180 is a top choice for the 2nd wing in downwind progression — handles chop better than pure ART
Pair with Advance fuselage and small Progressive rears for maximum carve

Fireball.name: Fireball
Fireball.tagline: F1 of downwind foiling — high camber speed machine
Fireball.character: High camber (new for AXIS), ~13 AR, very low stall speed despite high performance. Gets up early, fast top end. The benchmark for SUP downwind racing.
Fireball.who_its_for: SUP downwind racers, advanced riders wanting elite DW performance, step 3 in DW progression
Fireball.fuselage: Black Series (short to psycho short for most sizes). Fatty fuse + Fatty mast for 1500/1750.

Fireball 1160.highlight: Fred Bonnet's top pick for intermediate/advanced SUP DW
Fireball 1160.notes: Fred Bonnet: 'Unbelievable, effortless.' Yvon's favorite summer all-rounder. Starts in inverted or normal in water >16°C.

Fireball 1070.notes: Yvon: Duck starts with 2 steps almost every time. Daily driver for competitive riders.

Fireball 1000.notes: Yvon: FB 1000 benchmark for turning comparison with ART V2 939

Fireball 940.notes: Dmitry Evseev's favorite (75kg): 'Needs good waves/wind but flies when conditions cooperate'

Fireball 880.notes: Smaller, faster — for advanced riders in powered conditions

Fireball 1250.notes: Yvon: FB 1250 starts 10-15% easier than FB 1160. Good light wind backup.

Fireball 1350.notes: Mark Shinn: 'I prefer the 1350 for light winds, it has a lot of control. On the 1350 I use the psycho short fuselage as it pumps so well.'

Fireball 1500.highlight: Magic pumping machine — doubles pump time vs 1350
Fireball 1500.notes: Yvon Labarthe: 'Magic! Cruising speed 17-18 km/h. You will almost double your pumping time with the 1500 compared to the 1350.' REQUIRES Fatty mast for >85kg riders. Pair with Silly Short or Psycho Short fuse and Skinny 25.
Fireball 1500.weight_range: All weights. <85kg: Ultra Pro mast OK. >85kg: MUST use Fatty mast.

Fireball 1750.notes: Yvon: 'The FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone.' Ultra Pro mast bends under load — use Fatty mast. REQUIRES Fatty mast for >85kg.
Fireball 1750.weight_range: All weights. Fatty mast required for >85kg. Ultra Pro bends under this wing.

expert_quotes.quote: I prefer the 1350 for light winds, it has a lot of control. On the 1350 I use the psycho short as it pumps so well.
For the 1500 or 1750 you really need the fatty mast and fuse and they are slower...

expert_quotes.context: Top pick for intermediate/advanced SUP DW

expert_quotes.quote: FB 1500 v2 is magic! Glides straight, goes fast, turns pretty well. You will almost double your pumping time with the 1500 compared to the 1350.
The FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone.
I use the Fireball 1500 for pump/flat water/dock starts, paired with the 30 skinny tail. Advance Plus Ultrashort fuse.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026.

community_insights: Fireball has 25% MORE glide than equivalent ART V2 — use Fireball for pumping, ART V2 for turning
FB 1160 is Yvon's favorite summer all-rounder — rides all boats, downwind, super long downwind
Dmitry Evseev (75kg): FB 940 needs good waves/wind but flies when conditions cooperate
Nicolas Iten did nearly 5 hours on the Fireball 1750 around a lake — ~70km. (Kai Thompson, SurfFX interview Mar 2026)

Tempo.name: Tempo
Tempo.tagline: Next-gen ultra-high-aspect DW — Ti Link required
Tempo.character: Ultra-High Modulus carbon with Ti Link titanium fuselage. Higher aspect ratios, lower volumes. Greater hydrodynamic efficiency. Carries more speed and glide than equivalent Fireball.
Tempo.who_its_for: Intermediate to advanced DW/SUP riders wanting maximum efficiency. Leucate/racing conditions.
Tempo.fuselage: ONLY 70cm fuselage available — Short Advance Plus. No choice in length currently.
Tempo.fuselage_note: Mark Shinn: 'There is only 70cm available for the Tempo. We don't have a choice in that... Sometimes Adrian makes our life simple!'

Tempo 960.highlight: Approximate equivalent to Fireball 940 but more speed/glide
Tempo 960.notes: Philippe Axman: 'Plenty of speed to win a race.' Use 30 rear wing or below.

Tempo 1020.notes: Good when close to shore in offshore/windy swell
Tempo 1020.rider_feedback: Kai Thompson (80kg): Go-to travel wing, covers 10-30+ knots. "Insanely overpowered at 30 and they handle it." Pairs with 360 T25 tail, 72cm UHM mast, Advance Plus Ultrashort fuse.

Tempo 1090.highlight: Biggest Tempo — nothing glides better in the world
Tempo 1090.notes: Philippe Axman: 'More glide and speed than FB [1070].' Yvon: 'Nothing glides better in the world. 15-20% less effort than FB 1070.' REQUIRES integrated tail fuselage — normal stab adapter adds crazy brake.

Tempo 890.rider_feedback: Kai Thompson (80kg): High-wind option when it's nuking.

Tempo.tempo_sizing_guide: Tempo 960 ≈ Fireball 940 | Tempo 1090 ≈ Fireball 1070 (but with more glide and speed)
Tempo.rear_wing_guide: Tempo 1090 → 30 rear (DW), 35 rear (upwind). Above 35 = gain upwind but lose glide and pumping. Tempo 960 → 30 or below.
Tempo.stab_note: Tempo rear wings are REVISED SKINNIES MONOBLOC — not standard Skinny stabs. Order specifically for Tempo.

expert_quotes.quote: Tempos carry more speed and glide vs equivalent Fireball. Tempo 960 = plenty of speed to win a race.
expert_quotes.context: Tempo vs Fireball sizing for competition

expert_quotes.quote: You need to reduce rear wing size when you go longer fuselage. Otherwise hard to pump. Longer fuse + smaller tail = less drag = faster for racing downwind.
expert_quotes.context: Fuselage length and rear wing relationship

expert_quotes.quote: Tempo 1090: nothing glides better in the world. It looks like the FB 1070 with 200cm² less. Turns better, glides better. 15-20% less effort than FB 1070.
There is only 70cm available for the Tempo. We don't have a choice in that... Sometimes Adrian makes our life simple!

Adrian had to redesign the entire fuselage system for the Tempos — the wing design didn't fit any existing fuselage. After Hawaii they switched from weave carbon to unicarbon all the way through, gaining 24% more stiffness.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg, DW racer), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. Explaining Tempo development.

expert_quotes.quote: From the Tempos I'm getting consistency mostly. That's the biggest thing. You can be insanely overpowered on the 1020 at 30 knots and they handle it — there's no question in the riding ability because they're so stiff.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Tempo performance.

community_insights: Tempo not yet available in Europe warehouse as of early 2026 — order to home country first
Tempo transition from Fireball is 'not really hard — similar DNA' (Philippe Axman)
For Leucate competition: Tempo 960 and 1090 should cover most forecasts (Philippe Axman)

Surge.name: Surge
Surge.tagline: The wave weapon — back-foot surfing soul restored
Surge.character: Pure surf feel: instant response, shortboard snap, smooth rail-to-rail flow. First AXIS wing with moustache tips. Early lift despite relatively short span. Insane range from knee-high mush to outer-reef freight trains.
Surge.who_its_for: Wave riders first and foremost. Intermediate+ riders who want to shred, not just glide.
Surge.not_ideal_for: Flat water blasting (use ART V2 or Fireball instead), pure pump foiling

Surge 830.notes: Aurelien J (75kg): Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring. Uses Advance Ultrashort fuse, Skinny 360. 'Makes it feel alive and like it wants to turn.'
Surge 830.rider_feedback: Aurelien J (75kg): Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring Kai Thompson (80kg): Daily driver for surf, winging, downwind, Foil Drive. 80cm mast + Ultrashort fuse. Pairs with 345 tail.

Surge 950.rider_feedback: Kai Thompson (80kg): Best for dock starting competition. Won Boot Düsseldorf dock start event using this surf wing against dedicated race/starting foils.

Surge 1010.highlight: Mark Shinn's 'Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date'
Surge 1010.notes: Mark Shinn called it his favorite AXIS foil ever after testing. Great balance of lift and maneuverability.
Surge 1010.rider_feedback: Mark Shinn: 'Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date'

Surge.parawing_setup: Jerome recommends Skinny Surf 280 rear wing for parawing with Surge

expert_quotes.quote: Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date.
The Surge is designed to RIP waves and be easy to pump back out too.
The Surge 830 is my go-to for surf, winging, downwind, Foil Drive — I take it everywhere. The 830 and 950 cover me for everything including dock starts.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Surge versatility.

expert_quotes.quote: I used the Surge 950 for dock starting comp at Boot Düsseldorf — beat guys running dedicated starting foils with a surf wing. Made a few people think differently.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Surge in dock start competition.

expert_quotes.quote: If you're foil driving and have Axis, the Surge is awesome — really nice efficient glide cruising without the drag. Some surf setups are more draggy and less pumpy. This has way more freedom.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Surge with Foil Drive.

community_insights: Aurelien J (75kg) on Surge 830: Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring. Advance Ultrashort fuse + Skinny 360.
Jerome: Skinny Surf 280 is the go-to rear wing for parawing with Surge
Mark Shinn: 'Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date' — for the Surge 1010

Riders transitioning from ART V2 find they don't need as big a Surge as expected. The forward drive (from Fireball DNA) is the #1 unexpected selling point — not just the roll/turn feel. (SurfFX / Kai Thompson, Mar 2026)

fuselage_guide.overview: AXIS fuselages come in Red and Black series. Red for larger/thicker wings (BSC, PNG, Spitfire). Black for thinner/performance wings (ART, HPS, Fireball, Tempo, Surge).

red_series.description: 3/4 block aluminum, 4x 8mm bolts in T-formation. Strongest connection on market.
red_series.note: Essential for big span wings — strongest connection

sizes.Standard: Best for riders 240lb+ or largest wings
sizes.Short: Best for riders ~180lb, most common Red option

black_series.description: Thinner head, 2x 8mm + 2x 6mm bolts. Less drag for performance wings.

sizes.Ultrashort: Most popular intermediate/advanced — sweet spot of maneuverability and stability
sizes.Psycho Short: Extreme pump — used by Mark Shinn with Fireball 1350

advance_plus.description: Mast position 60mm further forward vs standard fuselage.
advance_plus.use_case: Advanced surf performance, tight turns on waves
advance_plus.best_with: ART wings, Spitfire, for maximum carve
advance_plus.note: NOT called 'Advance 20' — the correct name is 'Advance+'

ti_link.description: Aerospace-grade titanium, machined to absolute precision.
ti_link.notes: Designed specifically for Tempo but compatible with all AXIS setups. Premium option for ultimate performance.

fatty_fuse.description: Heavy-duty fuselage for 1500+ span wings and heavy riders.
fatty_fuse.use_case: Fireball 1500, Fireball 1750, heavy riders (85kg+) pushing limits
fatty_fuse.note: Mark Shinn: 'For the 1500 or 1750 you really need the fatty mast and fuse'

principles.longer_fuse: More stability, easier for beginners, better for big wings
principles.shorter_fuse: More maneuverable, advanced riders, tighter turns
principles.popular_choice: Ultrashort is the most popular intermediate/advanced fuselage length
principles.rear_wing_relationship: Philippe Axman: Longer fuse requires smaller rear wing to maintain pump efficiency. Longer fuse + smaller tail = same pitch stability + faster racing speed.

19mm_aluminum.name: 19mm Aluminum
19mm_aluminum.character: Gold standard, stiffest aluminum on market
19mm_aluminum.who_its_for: All disciplines, essential for larger span wings. Best all-around choice.
19mm_aluminum.notes: Most reliable, most durable. Start here.

power_carbon.name: Power Carbon
power_carbon.who_its_for: Advanced riders, big guys, biggest wings, those pushing limits
power_carbon.notes: Essential for ART series to get proper performance. 'More positive feel with immediate response'
power_carbon.when_to_upgrade: When you feel flex limiting your performance, or riding ART series

ultra_pro_carbon.name: Ultra Pro / PRO Ultra High Modulus Carbon
ultra_pro_carbon.who_its_for: Elite performance, when you want the absolute best
ultra_pro_carbon.notes: Yvon Labarthe: Ultra Pro slides super well but bends under Fireball 1750 load. Use Fatty mast for 1500+.
ultra_pro_carbon.cost: Outrageously expensive — for those who must have the best

fatty_mast.name: Power Carbon FATTY / Fatty Mast
fatty_mast.character: Extra torsional stiffness for extreme loads
fatty_mast.who_its_for: 1500+ span wings (Fireball 1500, 1750) AND heavy riders (85kg+) pushing limits
fatty_mast.critical_note: REQUIRED for Fireball 1500/1750 if rider is >85kg. Ultra Pro mast bends under 1750. Yvon confirmed.
fatty_mast.common_misconception: Not just for 'winging' — for high-load situations regardless of discipline

carbon_integrated_foil_drive.name: Carbon Integrated Foil Drive
carbon_integrated_foil_drive.notes: Special mast accommodating Foil Drive unit. Battery sits in board cutout.

notes: Fatty mast system: 95mm profile mast cut down — fatter from base all the way through. MUST pair with fatty fuselage (wider mounting). Recommended for heavier riders (80kg+) on Fireballs. Lighter riders (sub-80kg) can use standard mast. (Kai Thompson / SurfFX, Mar 2026)

steps.description: Learn to catch bumps. Big span, forgiving, catches small wind swell. James Casey's recommendation for DW beginners.
Step up to higher aspect. ART Pro 1201 for speed-focused, Spitfire 1180 for those wanting chop forgiveness.
Elite downwind performance. Fireball 1160 = Fred Bonnet's top pick. ART Pro 951 = Kai Lenny's race wing.
The next level. Ultra-high-aspect, Ti Link fuselage, maximum efficiency. For serious competitors.

beginner_winging_80kg.title: Beginner Winging — ~80kg Rider
beginner_winging_80kg.notes: Most forgiving setup. Focus on getting up and controlling altitude before worrying about performance.

beginner_winging_heavy.title: Beginner Winging — 90kg+ Rider
beginner_winging_heavy.notes: Extra lift and stability for heavier riders. Red Standard fuselage essential for this weight.

intermediate_allround.title: Intermediate All-Round
intermediate_allround.notes: Speed and versatility. HPS 880 requires good body mechanics to manage pitch.

advanced_winging_prone.title: Advanced Winging/Prone
advanced_winging_prone.notes: Maximum performance. ART needs Power Carbon to perform properly.

advanced_surf.title: Advanced Surf (Wave Riding)
advanced_surf.notes: Surge is purpose-built for waves. Jerome recommends Surf Skinny 280 for parawing specifically.

downwind_beginner.title: Downwind — Beginner
downwind_beginner.notes: Big span to catch small bumps. Don't rush to smaller wings — stay here until you catch bumps consistently.

downwind_intermediate.title: Downwind — Intermediate
downwind_intermediate.notes: Fred Bonnet: FB 1160 is 'unbelievable, effortless' for intermediate/advanced SUP DW.

downwind_advanced.title: Downwind — Advanced/Racing
downwind_advanced.fuselage: Ti Link 70cm (Tempo) or Black Psycho Short (Fireball)
downwind_advanced.mast: Ultra Pro Carbon or Power Carbon
downwind_advanced.notes: Yvon: Tempo 1090 = 15-20% less effort than FB 1070. Philippe: Tempo 960 and 1090 cover most Leucate race forecasts.

pump_foiling_endurance.title: Pump Foiling / Endurance
pump_foiling_endurance.mast: Fatty mast for 1500/1750. Ultra Pro if <85kg and using 1500.
pump_foiling_endurance.notes: Yvon: FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone. Silly Short or Psycho Short fuselage for maximum pump efficiency.

Fireball.text: FB 1500 v2 is magic! You will almost double your pumping time with the 1500 compared to the 1350. Cruising speed 17-18 km/h.
The FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone.
FB 1160: I rode it all summer, it's my favorite foil for riding. Did almost all boats, downwind, super long downwind.
Fireball 1160 — unbelievable, effortless. Top pick for intermediate/advanced SUP downwind.
FB 940 is my favorite — needs good waves/wind but flies when conditions cooperate.

Tempo.text: Tempo 1090: nothing glides better in the world. It looks like the FB 1070 with 200cm² less. 15-20% less effort than FB 1070.
Tempo 960 = plenty of speed to win a race. Tempos carry more speed and glide than equivalent Fireball.

Surge.text: Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date. The Surge is designed to RIP waves and be easy to pump back out.
Surge 830: Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring. Uses Advance Ultrashort fuse, Skinny 360.

PNG.text: Learned on PNG 1310 & 1300. Went from standard to Advanced+ fuselage and it made the foils so much more responsive for such large spans.

ART V2.text: With ART V2 1099, I have 25% less glide than FB 1160 — Fireball wins for pumping. ART v2 turns 5-10% better rail-to-rail.
ART v2 879 behind a boat in towing — ça tourne grave! (turns like crazy). For wing, everyone loves ART v2.

common_pitfalls.description: Some riders struggle with pitch control on the HPS 880 — over-correcting up/down. This is normal early on. Focus on smooth, gradual body movements rather than reactive corrections.
common_pitfalls.solution: Give it time. Use Advance fuselage and proper mast. Most riders get past this within 5-10 sessions.

common_pitfalls.description: Standard ART wings struggle in turbulent/choppy conditions — they're designed for smooth water. Using ART in chop leads to frustration.
common_pitfalls.solution: Use ART V2 or Spitfire for choppy conditions. Save ART for smooth water days.

common_pitfalls.description: Using a Black fuselage with PNG or large BSC (or Red fuselage with ART/Fireball). Each fuselage type is engineered for specific wing geometries.
common_pitfalls.solution: Red for PNG/BSC/large Spitfire. Black for HPS/ART/Fireball/Tempo/Surge.

common_pitfalls.description: Yvon Labarthe confirmed the Ultra Pro mast bends significantly under the Fireball 1750. If you're over 85kg, this is unsafe.
common_pitfalls.solution: Use Fatty mast for Fireball 1500/1750 if you're >85kg.

common_pitfalls.issue: Tempo with wrong rear wing adapter
common_pitfalls.description: Using a normal stab adapter with Skinny rear wings on Tempo adds significant drag ('crazy brake' — Yvon).
common_pitfalls.solution: MUST use Tempo's integrated tail fuselage. Order Tempo-specific revised Skinny monobloc stabs.

common_pitfalls.description: Moving to high-aspect wings (ART Pro, Fireball) before consistently catching bumps on PNG 1300.
common_pitfalls.solution: Stay on PNG 1300 until you consistently link bumps. Then step to Spitfire 1180 or ART Pro 1201.
